title: Virus Containment
statement:
A virus spreads through a grid. Each second it infects every fresh cell (`1`) adjacent (4-directional) to an already-infected cell (`2`). Walls (`-1`) block spread. Empty cells (`0`) can never be infected. Return the number of seconds until no more cells can be infected, or -1 if some fresh cells can never be reached.

**Input:** First line: n m. Next n lines: m space-separated values (0=empty, 1=fresh, 2=infected, -1=wall).
**Output:** Seconds to infect all reachable cells, or -1.

examples.explanation: Takes 4 seconds for the virus to reach all fresh cells.
Cell (2,0) has no path to the infected cell.
Infected at (0,1) spreads to (0,0) in 1 second.
